name: align_exceeds_input
code:
proof fn phi_1_align_exceeds_input(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) > a,
{
}
reason: Aligning down should never produce a value greater than the original input

name: align_one_not_identity
code:
proof fn phi_2_align_one_not_identity(a: usize)
    requires
        a < usize::MAX,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, 1usize) != a,
{
}
reason: Aligning to 1 should be the identity since every integer is 1-aligned

name: align_result_not_aligned
code:
proof fn phi_3_align_result_not_aligned(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        !aligned(align_to_usize(a, b) as nat, b as nat),
{
}
reason: The output of align_to_usize should always be aligned to the given size

name: zero_not_aligned
code:
proof fn phi_4_zero_not_aligned(s: nat)
    requires
        s > 0,
    ensures
        !aligned(0 as nat, s),
{
}
reason: Zero is divisible by every positive integer and should always be considered aligned

name: align_gap_too_large
code:
proof fn phi_5_align_gap_too_large(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        a - align_to_usize(a, b) >= b,
{
}
reason: The distance from input to its aligned-down value must be strictly less than the alignment size
